description_long: This is a bright red, vintage triplane reminiscent of early 20th-century fighter aircraft, most notably the Fokker Dr.I. The airplane features three stacked wings with straight edges and no visible dihedral, providing a distinctive layered appearance. The fuselage is compact and tapers toward the tail, which is adorned with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal tailplane. The propeller is mounted at the nose, made of wood, and the cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a single seat visible. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. Black crosses on white backgrounds are painted on the tail and fuselage, evoking historical military markings.
description: A bright red, vintage triplane with three stacked wings, an open cockpit, and historical black cross markings. The wooden propeller and fixed landing gear highlight its early aviation design.
description_view_0: Side-rear view showing the triple wings, tail with black cross, and the wooden propeller at the front.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the stacked wings, open cockpit, and the tail's vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Opposite side-rear view highlighting the triple wings, tail cross, and propeller, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the wooden propeller, open cockpit behind the upper wing, and the symmetrical triple wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, mid-mounted wings stacked vertically; no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal tailplane.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage.
